channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: WWE NXT Champion Joe Hendry Reveals Indie Game He Plays Most
item.content:encoded:
Joe Hendry is the current WWE NXT Champion. He’s also a viral star thanks to his earworm of a theme song and amusing antics, appearing whenever anyone says his name. However, like most WWE Superstars, Hendry is also a gamer. Now, he’s revealing his favorite indie game.

During an interview with Windows Central’s Jez Corden, Hendry revealed that he has a PC, a Steam Deck, an Xbox, and a PlayStation 5. However, with his busy road schedule, he doesn’t get much time to deep-dive into video games. When he does, the genre that he chooses might not be what you think of when you think of the comedic sports entertainer.

Joe Hendry Loves A City-Builder

Joe Hendry revealed that he enjoys city-building games. As such, when he has some free time, he will try to build the village in a game like Kingdoms and Castles.

He also revealed that his favorite indie game is They Are Billions. He calls the game “amazing” and says it’s the one he puts most of his time into.

“So for me, if I’m just by myself, I like city-building games. If I’ve got a spare 20 minutes, I’ll just build a village in Kingdoms and Castles or something like that. I love indie games like that. They Are Billions. That’s the game I’ve put the most time into. It can be super frustrating … one zombie gets through, and the whole thing’s gone. But yes, amazing game,” he said. He also revealed that he had been a big fan of Final Fantasy 7 and the Metal Gear series.

These days, Hendry has worked himself into a position where he will be playable in a video game. Joe Hendry is playable on day one as the pre-order bonus for the upcoming WWE 2K26 title. That game will launch on March 6 for those who pre-ordered premium editions. The game will launch globally on March 13.

Are you surprised by Joe Hendry’s favorite game? Are you looking forward to playing as Joe in WWE 2K26? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Nintendo Adding Classic Mario Games to Switch Online for MAR10 Day
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo has announced some of what it will be doing to celebrate Mario Day (MAR10 Day). Nintendo Switch Online subscribers will get access to 3 classic titles, including a couple of Virtual Boy games that were not released when that platform was added to the NSO service.

Mario Day takes place next Tuesday, March 10. Nintendo announced on March 3 that it will be releasing Mario’s Tennis and Mario Clash on the Virtual Boy service from Nintendo Switch Online. Also, the original Mario vs. Donkey Kong will be available on the Game Boy Advance service on NSO.

Mario’s Tennis was actually the first tennis game to feature Nintendo’s mascot. Released in 1995, it took advantage of the Virtual Boy’s depth perception for some unique gameplay. Mario Clash was a take on the original Mario Bros. game, with Mario using a Powerball to knock out enemies.

Mario vs. Donkey Kong is simply a part of the original Game Boy Advance release. The game was remade for Nintendo Switch in 2024 with updated graphics.

What Else Could Be Coming on MAR10 Day

Plenty of Nintendo fans are delusionally hopeful that this year’s Mario Day could finally be the day Nintendo shows off a glimpse of the next 3D Mario game. It’s been almost 9 years since the release of Super Mario Odyssey on the original Nintendo Switch, and this is the longest gap between Mario releases.

However, it’s more likely that if anything more is to come from Nintendo, it could be a trailer for the upcoming Super Mario Galaxy Movie. The movie will be released in less than a month on April 1. So far, trailers have shown off the likes of Yoshi, Birdo, Mouser, and more. Still, there’s the chance that Nintendo might want to provide at least one more reveal ahead of the movie’s theatrical release.

Previous MAR10 Days have also seen certain Mario titles go on sale on the Nintendo eShop. Overall, it remains to be seen how much Nintendo will do to celebrate MAR10 Day. If you want to celebrate, you can go to GameStop dressed up like Nintendo’s top Superstar and earn some store credit. If you’re in New York City, you can even try to break a record. Learn more about that here.

Are you hoping for anything in particular to be announced on MAR10 Day? Are you looking forward to the Super Mario Galaxy Movie? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Marathon Rated By ESRB Ahead of Launch
item.content:encoded:
Bungie’s upcoming extraction shooter, Marathon, has been officially rated T for Teen by the ESRB. The rating comes just one day before the game goes live on Thursday, March 5, 2026. The T rating is due to the following factors: Language, violence, and animated blood.

Ahead of its release, Marathon experienced a server slam on Thursday, February 26. At its peak, 143,621 players were testing the game. Although that number quickly declined, Marathon still drew significant attention and outperformed titles such as Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 and Battlefield 6. Additionally, ARC Raiders viewership on Twitch hit an all-time low in the early hours of the Marathon Server Slam.

However, within six hours, the peak player count dropped by about 30,000. Of course, the beta test encountered some bugs and glitches, including issues the team here at Insider Gaming faced.

What to Expect from Marathon

With Marathon set to launch on March 5, it’s time to consider what to expect from the upcoming shooter. The first three seasons of the game provide a rough outline of what to expect. It’s already known that Season 2 will begin in June, be named Nightfall, and run through August, at which point Season 3 is expected to begin and run through November.

At the start of each season, all players will have their gear, contract progression, faction progression, and player level wiped. That’s right, each season is planned as a complete fresh start. Bear in mind that just because there’s an outline for up to three seasons doesn’t guarantee the game will resonate with players immediately.

Truthfully, this is a tough period for live service games. Highguard was featured prominently at the 2025 Game Awards and will shut down its servers one week after Marathon launches, having not even stayed online for two full months.

Amazon’s King of Meat is another example of a live service game with a short lifespan. That game had a major marketing campaign behind it and was hyped by multiple creators, including MrBeast. Even still, that game will be shutting down in April.

To learn more about the roadmap ahead for Marathon, click here. Insider Gaming will continue to provide coverage of the extraction shooter following its launch.

Are you looking forward to playing Marathon? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: First Two Wrestlers Set for WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass Season 3
item.content:encoded:
The first two WWE Superstars have been announced for the third season of the WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass. One is a freshly returned WWE Superstar, and the other is a legend who has held numerous titles.

Matt Cardona, formerly known as Zack Ryder, is making his return to WWE 2K games as part of the third season of the Ringside Pass. He announced his return in a video on social media. The last time Cardona was in a game was WWE 2K20 in 2019. Cardona was released by WWE in 2020 and recently returned to WWE.

While he was away, he was a playable character in the game Retromania Wrestling.

WWE 2K26 Will Feature A ‘Loose Cannon’ As Well

In addition to Matt Cardona, WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass Season 3 will also include the legendary Brian Pillman. Pillman’s inclusion was announced by his son, current WWE NXT Superstar Lexis King. King mentioned that he was excited for players to play as both him and his father, and hopefully make them a tag team.

Brian Pillman hasn’t been featured in a WWE video game in 10 years. His last appearance was in WWE 2K17. Pillman, who passed away in 1997, made his video game debut in WCW Superbrawl on the Super Nintendo back in 1994. He would also be featured in the 2000s Legends of Wrestling video game series.

WWE 2K26 Sees the Return of Demolition As Well

In addition to these announcements, it was revealed during the most recent episode of Monday Night Raw that Ax and Smash, Demolition, would be featured in the second season of the WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass. This will be the first time Ax and Smash have been featured in a game since WWE ’12 in 2011.

Like Cardona, Ax, and Smash are in Retromania Wrestling. That game is available now on PlayStation, Xbox, and Nintendo Switch consoles. It’s also available on PC via Steam.

According to Visual Concepts, Season 2 of the Ringside Pass will be available in April before WrestleMania 42, which begins on April 18. Season 3 of the Ringside Pass will be available in June. Learn more about the choice behind the new DLC system at this link.

WWE 2K26 hits Early Access on March 6 for those who purchased premium editions. It will release globally on March 13.

Will you be playing WWE 2K26? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP
